name: Day Night Cycle
description: Advanced day-night cycle system with realistic atmospheric scattering that simulates the passage of time by rotating the sun and updating sky appearance. Attach to a DirectionalLight to create dynamic lighting conditions that change from sunrise through noon, sunset, and night. Uses three.js SkyMesh for physically-based sky rendering.
documentation:
## Day Night Cycle
Simulate a day-night cycle by rotating a directional light.

### Settings
- **Enable Sun Rotation** - Toggle the cycle on/off
- **Initial Time (Hours)** - Starting time of day (0-24)
- **Rotation Speed** - How fast time passes
- **Pause Rotation** - Freeze the current time

### Tips
- Attach to a **Directional Light** object only
- Set initial time to 6 for sunrise, 12 for noon, 18 for sunset
- Use low rotation speed for subtle ambient changes
- Pause rotation to lock the lighting at a specific time
author: stemstudio

enableDayNightCycle.name: Enable Sun Rotation
enableDayNightCycle.description: Toggle the day-night cycle on or off. When enabled, the sun rotates and the sky appearance updates over time.

initialTimeHours.name: Initial Time (Hours)
initialTimeHours.description: Starting time of day in 24-hour format. Use 6 for sunrise, 12 for noon, or 18 for sunset.

rotationSpeed.name: Rotation Speed
rotationSpeed.description: How fast time passes in the cycle. Higher values make the sun move faster across the sky.

isPaused.name: Pause Rotation
isPaused.description: Freeze the cycle at the current time of day. Useful for locking lighting to a specific moment.
